summary: Smoking Alligator jam, soaring Dew solo, tight Lovelight flow
blurb: Second set erupts with a smoking Alligator jam that weaves into an hour‑plus Turn On Your Lovelight/Not Fade Away sequence. Jerry delivers soaring solos on Morning Dew while Pigpen’s organ on Lovelight adds a Janis‑Joplin‑like intensity. Soundboard recordings are excellent, capturing the band at a relaxed yet tight Fillmore West performance.
review: June 6, 1970 at the Fillmore West finds the Dead in a wonderfully relaxed mood that quickly turns into high‑octane improvisation. The first set is solid but unremarkable; the magic truly begins with Morning Dew, where Jerry’s tone sings and his soaring solos lift the entire band. From there the second set launches into a smoking Alligator jam that segues flawlessly into an hour‑plus Turn On Your Lovelight → Not Fade Away → Turn On Your Lovelight marathon, showcasing Pigpen’s organ on Lovelight as a cosmic male/female blend reminiscent of Janis Joplin. Bob Weir’s rhythm work and vocal highlight on Friend Of The Devil shine in the acoustic fragment, while the twin drummers lock‑in with thunderous precision throughout, only slipping a beat on the Uncle John’s Band outro. Across all four soundboard sources the audio is crisp—especially the bear‑latvala capture, noted for reduced hiss and magnificent clarity—making this show a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s early‑70s exploratory peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Alligator jam, Lovelight/Not Fade Away sequence, Jerry’s solos, and Pigpen’s organ. No consistent negative comments appear; only minor critiques (e.g., Uncle John’s Band outro) are isolated. This strong consensus yields a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with reduced hiss, magnificent clarity, and superb balance that highlights the band’s relaxed yet tight performance.

key_highlights: Smoking Alligator jam that launches the second set
Epic Turn On Your Lovelight → Not Fade Away → Turn On Your Lovelight marathon
Pigpen’s organ on Lovelight described as a cosmic Janis‑Joplin blend
Jerry’s soaring Morning Dew solos and tight interplay with Bob

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring guitar on the Alligator jam and Morning Dew, tight interplay with Bob; brief mix drop‑out on Uncle John's Band outro but overall stellar.
band_performance.Bob: Provides strong rhythmic support, especially after the Alligator drums; vocal performance on Friend Of The Devil is highlighted as a standout.
band_performance.Brent: Pigpen’s organ on Turn On Your Lovelight adds a cosmic male/female power blend, likened to Janis Joplin, enhancing the jam dynamics.
band_performance.Mickey: Locked‑in, powerful drumming throughout the second set, driving the Alligator and post‑Alligator jams.
band_performance.Billy: Thunderous precision on most of the show; a slight off‑beat feel noted only on the Uncle John's Band finale.
